In a word ... "BORING". You will spend 95% of your time searching for non-existant enemy traffic. You can sit for 12 hours just outside a major port waiting for some traffic in or out, but there is none. The controls are terrible. Do you want to put on 10 degrees of port rudder ? Not going to happen in SH5 ... you can have 5 degrees or full rudder and that's all. No compass (seriously), wth is up with that ? I spent my years (irl) flopping around the ocean watching the horizon go up and down. I'm quite sure that's not entertainment. Buy SH3 instead, its actually fun. (PS. They can't spell Inverness either).

By far one of the best Subsim games I have ever seen or played, i wish there were more Subsims like this but there are not. This game is a bit tedius but too much for me. I like it because its full 3d, you play a part in the crew's morale, you can control the deck guns, and the realism setting are amazing. If you like Subsimsyou should at the very least try this one.
Best navy game so far!
I know that some things in it are not so great as it was in SH3 but in my opinion it a great game for a naval sim. 7/10.

This game is an incomplete piece of crap.  Beautiful in theory, its final execution is spectacularly underperforming and inept in almost every way.  More importantly, the host of bugs that leave the final product quality trapped in what should rightly be the early development phase are now permanent, since Ubisoft has long since stopped providing any fixes or customer support for it.  I don't blame them; no one likes to be repeatedly reminded about their shoddy workmanship.  On the other hand, they shouldn't be asking money for it either.

Thanks to insanely die-hard fans of the series, there are numerous mods available online to improve the game.  Still, you shouldn't have to clean up someone else's mess.  Stick to SH 3 and 4, two really nice games for this genre.
Worst game ever.

I waited until the price dropped enough to take a chance on a game that missed on the reviews a couple years ago.  But SH3 doesnt work on Windows 8.1 so I had to try it.  Well, I'm disappointed in it enough to want to through out this crappy Windows 8 and install my old XP 64, just so i can play a decent WWII sub game.  I thought that by now the mods and patches would've made SH5 at least a halfway enjoyable game....not nearly anything to even try to explain to you....I'm just disappointed that the developers ruined the once fantastic franchise.

When someone asks me about my favorite RPG I tell them that Silent Hunter 5 is my fave. Why? I mean it isn't wizards and warriors running around. But hey your crew does kind of level up. You do get phat lewtz and new gear. But the main reason I say I enjoyed this game the most as an RPG is that when you play it you can really dive into the idea of actually being a submarine captain. How so? Well you can play it entirely from the first person, so you can really immerse yourself in the situation. I've found myself more involved with my crew and my current situation in a game of SH5 than any of the big studio rpgs. I mean sure I couldn't walk around the ship and then romance each crew member after a mission, but hey you still start to project things onto them by the end of a campaign.

Anyways, pretty great game.

(By the bye, it does say I haven't played it. I played the heck out of it on another account then picked it up via steam sale on this one too. So hey.)

I am very upset it runs for 2mins in windowed and then crash and there isnt an update grrrrrrr fix the game before u start selling it please. I loved number 3 but can't play it if it wont load........

This is an excellent submarine simulator, but being an excellent simulator is always a two-edged sword.

Combat is fun and wel modelled, and the graphics are pretty. Wreaking havoc amongst enemy convoys is incredibly satisying, but getting to the battle in the first place is a very tedious process. The game does include a time dilation feature, but the autopilot will have serious difficulty navigating at higher speeds, unless you're in deep open water. This game will appeal to hardcore simulator buffs, but if you're turned off by the idea of spending hours just finding something to shoot, this is not the game for you.

Also, this game requires a internet connection and the Ubisoft online launcher to play. When I buy a game through Steam, I already have a layer of internet DRM. I don't appreciate it when a title requires me to install a second internet DRM launcher to play it.

At the end of the day, while I enjoyed wrecking convoys, the time it took to find and intercept the convoys plus the extra Ubisoft DRM has prevented me from picking the game up again after my initial binge.

Why did you make a super complex game but offer no tutorial other than "hurr aim torpedo shooty shipy"? Why is this like Spore where you have to do everything? Why do I need to use the deck gun manually? What's the difference between HE and AP shells for the deck gun? They seem to be the exact same thing. Why do I need to run to the bow of the ship to use the special torpedo ability, then run back to the command deck to fire it? Why can't I just use my officer's intercom to ask him to use his special ability? Why was the game released in this condition? AND WHY DOES IT TAKE 3 TORPEDOS TO SINK A FUCKING CARGO SHIP? IT'S LIKE THE SMALLEST ONE TOO.

Under any other circumstance of a game having this level of DRM protection, I wouldn't buy it.  But Silent Hunter 5 is by far and away the best World War 2 sub sim out there, and has been for years.  Its worth it, even the hassle.  Effects and environment are stellar.  Realism as well is second to none in the genre.
